title: The Big Picture 2023 On Our Radar
section_path: The Big Picture 2023 On Our Radar
section_path_text: The Big Picture 2023 On Our Radar
summary:
Things to look out for
As we start the new year, we look into some of the factors that may play a role in determining freight rates for the year ahead. By Mark Nugent

Iron ore and steel
With news of further support policies for the Chinese property market being implemented, the iron ore price in China has rebounded to $123.2/t on the Dalian Commodity Exchange, an increase of 39.7% from the lows at the start of November. While the move prices in a healthy improvement in demand from the Covid-stricken depths of 2H22, we do not expect Chinese steel production to surpass its previous peak.

children.title: Things to look out for
children.summary: As we start the new year, we look into some of the factors that may play a role in determining freight rates for the year ahead.

children.title: Iron ore and steel
children.summary: With news of further support policies for the Chinese property market being implemented, the iron ore price in China has rebounded to $123.2/t on the Dalian Commodity Exchange, an increase of 39.7% from the lows at the start of November. While the move prices in a healthy improvement in demand from the Covid-stricken depths of 2H22, we do not expect Chinese steel production to surpass its previous peak.

children.title: China re-opening
children.summary: Many welcomed the sight of China lifting Covid-19 restrictions in early December as FFA prices moved to the upside. Though China is now quickly reopening, strong economic activity will not return overnight, particularly as the country deals with the subsequent surge in cases.

children.title: Coal headwinds
children.summary: The largest story breaking in the new year has been a reported partial lifting of China's unofficial ban of Australian coal which has been in place since 2020. It is also solely open for a handful of state-owned utilities, implying trade will not return to pre-ban levels.

children.title: West Africa's growing importance
children.summary: The continued strength in Guinean bauxite trade in 2022 was one of the few trades that hit new heights last year. Bulk carriers loaded over 100m tonnes of bauxite in Guinea for the first time in 2022, increasing by 15.2% YoY.
